hero.title: Field notes from the network.
hero.dek: Source-backed chapter updates, guild reports, event recaps, and public program notes.
hero.body: Stories are the place for supported narrative, not unsupported metrics. If research supports a direction but not a precise number, the story should keep it as qualitative copy.

sections.title: Start with approved field work.
sections.dek: Nigeria Water Cup, Tech & Sun, Earth Week, Phangan environmental monitoring, Brasil GG20 climate milestones, the Garden, and the new Paragraph-backed network and Dev Guild stories now form the first published source-backed wave.
sections.body: Keep featured stories limited to public source URLs, clear steward-approved evidence, and `published` status.

sections.title: Follow the public field notes.
sections.dek: Newsletter copy can be public, but drafts from Drive remain internal until approved.
sections.body: Use Paragraph or another public endpoint as the outgoing link until a newsletter form provider is chosen.

sections.title: Publish a chapter or guild update.
sections.dek: Submission data should go to a private/moderated flow. Keystatic only stores approved public stories.
sections.body: The public CTA can exist before the private workflow is implemented, but it should not write raw drafts or contact data into public content.

topicSpotlight.blurb: Green Goods, Greenpill Nigeria's Water Cup proof, Brasil's KarmaGAP milestones, Tech & Sun's public infrastructure thread, PGSP, and the public Impact Framework discussion show how the network is turning local work into verifiable public claims.

translations.label: Greenpilled and Impact DAOs translation work is source-backed for Brasil, but final publication copy needs steward review.

seo.title: Stories · Greenpill Network
seo.description: Read source-backed Greenpill chapter updates, guild reports, public program notes, event recaps, and field stories.
